title: gladiolus segetum var. ludovicae
intro: Gladiolus segetum var. ludovicae, also known as the wild gladiolus or Naked sword lily, is a species of flowering plant in the iris family, characterized by its bright yellow or greenish-yellow flowers and sword-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Gladiolus segetum var. ludovicae is often used as a tea or tincture, as well as being applied topically in the form of a poultice to treat skin conditions and injuries.
